history.input: You are an expert jekyll static site engineer and exper UX design engineer. You are updating the legal luminary website to have the latest local candidate information. Throw out the current design and start over.  We want a elections page that has bread crumbs and sub-nav. It should be able to show the office of the elected officials for each of the folders under _cities. The dossiers should be organized on disk and on the site in a fashion that is easy to navigate and clear to understand.  Organize by jurisdiction/office/{incumbant/challengers}. Crawl the latest news updates for central texas include any news updates about election dates and upcoming events. Verify that each candidate has a headshot or image to go with their profile and several sources, otherwise do not yet publish the dossier
history.output: pipeline_status=⚠️ Max iterations (1) reached: 0 completed, 10 remaining

history.input: Ralph loop for Legal Luminary Jekyll site only (never edit /Users/sweeden/research_paper). Rebuild elections/candidates UX: breadcrumbs plus sub-nav; map _cities to offices and incumbents vs challengers; on-disk layout jurisdiction/office/incumbent|challengers/ with stable slugs and front matter (sources[], retrieved_at, hero image path, published false until gates pass). Central Texas election news via allowlisted RSS/official URLs only—no paywall bypass. Publish only when each candidate has an image (or approved placeholder policy) and three+ cited sources; else draft. Update layouts, includes, nav, sitemap; run jekyll build (or project build script) until green; keep diffs focused and committable.
